channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: 2 Ukrainian women are pulled alive from rubble hours after Russian missile strike
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Rescue crews pulled two women from rubble several hours after a Russian missile struck a medical clinic in southern Ukraine, killing nine people and wounding 22 others, authorities said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Macron seeks political deal to name a new prime minister and restore France's stability
item.description: A spokesperson for France's outgoing government says President Emmanuel Macron is seeking a political deal that would allow him to both name a new prime minister and “guarantee the stability of the country.”.
item.dc:creator: Sylvie Corbet, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Senators call on Biden to brief upper chamber on efforts to return Austin Tice from Syria
item.description: In a bipartisan push, senators have called on President Biden to brief the upper chamber to provide specific details on efforts underway to secure the release of Austin Tice from captivity in Syria.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea condemns South Korea as 'fascist dictatorship' after martial law scare
item.description: North Korean dictator Kim Jong Un denounced South Korea as a 'fascist dictatorship' following President Yoon Suk Yeol's failed attempt to impose martial law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Scholz sets Germany on course for an early election as he requests a confidence vote next week
item.description: Chancellor Olaf Scholz has formally set Germany on course for an early election by requesting a confidence vote in parliament next week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Romania's pro-Western parties agree to form a majority government after political turmoil
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Romania’s pro-European parties agreed Wednesday to form a majority government made up of groupings that were traditionally on opposite sides and shutting out far-right nationalists who made significant gains in the election on Dec. 1.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supporters of Bangladesh Nationalist Party hold protest march as tensions continue with India
item.description: Thousands of members of youth and student bodies belonging to the Bangladesh Nationalist Party have held a road march procession from Bangladesh’s capital toward its eastern border with India to protest against an attack on a diplomatic mission and alleged desecration of Bangladeshi flags in India.
item.dc:creator: Julhas Alam, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mike Huckabee 'commends' Biden support for Israel's self-defense
item.description: Israel said Tuesday it had bombed more than 350 military sites in Syria during the previous 48 hours.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, The Hill

item.title: Israeli strikes on Gaza kill at least 29, Palestinian medics say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli strikes pounded the Gaza Strip overnight and into Wednesday, hitting a home where displaced people were sheltering in the isolated north and a built-up refugee camp. At least 29 people were killed, according to Palestinian health officials.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Brazilian beef and leather companies fall short in tackling deforestation, a study finds
item.description: BRASILIA, Brazil (AP) — The next United Nations climate conference, COP30, will be held in Belem, the capital of an Amazon region where widespread deforestation mainly driven by cattle farming has turned the surrounding rainforest from a vital carbon sink into a significant carbon source.
item.dc:creator: FABIANO MAISONNAVE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taiwan demands that China end its military activity in nearby waters
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Taiwan demanded Wednesday that China end its ongoing military activity in nearby waters, which it said is unilaterally undermining peace and stability and disrupting international shipping and trade.
item.dc:creator: TAIJING WU and KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An explosion in the Afghan capital kills the Taliban refugee minister
item.description: A suicide bombing in the Afghan capital has killed the Taliban refugee minister and two others in the most brazen attack on a member of the Taliban inner circle since they returned to power three years ago.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ex-defense chief in South Korea tried to kill himself after being arrested over martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s previous defense minister was stopped from attempting suicide while in detention over last week’s martial law declaration, officials said Wednesday, as President Yoon Suk Yeol’s office resisted a police attempt to search the compound.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea's former defense minister attempted suicide after he was arrested over martial law probe
item.description: South Korea’s former defense minister, Kim Yong Hyun, attempted suicide while in detention over the president's martial law declaration, but officials were able to stop him.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Lebanese man who spent 32 years in Syrian prisons is welcomed home
item.description: CHEKKA, Lebanon (AP) — Suheil Hamwi spent 32 years in a Syrian prison, and now, after an offensive by insurgents that toppled the government of Bashar Assad, he's finally returned to his home in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strike on home in northern Gaza kills 19 people, Palestinian medics say
item.description: Palestinian medical officials say an Israeli strike on a home sheltering displaced people in the northern Gaza Strip has killed at least 19.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Humanitarian aid to northern Gaza mostly blocked for the last 2 months, UN says
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Humanitarian aid to n orth Gaza, where Israel launched a ground offensive on Oct. 6, has largely been blocked for the past 66 days, the United Nations said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: These Palestinians disappeared after encounters with Israeli troops in Gaza
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Reem Ajour says she last saw her husband and then 4-year-old daughter in March, when Israeli soldiers raided a family home in northern Gaza.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, SARAH EL DEEB and JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US Navy defeats Houthi attacks in Gulf of Aden for second time in weeks
item.description: U.S. Navy destroyers USS Stockdale (DDG 106) and USS O’Kane (DDG 77) successfully defeated a range of Houthi-launched weapons while transiting the Gulf of Aden for the second time.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rights group demands probe of ex-governor, 2 army officers in Congo for possible humanitarian crimes
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — Amnesty International called for an investigation Wednesday into a former governor and two top army officials in Congo for “possible crimes against humanity” during a deadly crackdown on demonstrators last year protesting the U.N. peacekeeping mission in the country.
item.dc:creator: MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu vows to challenge 'absurd' corruption charges as he takes the stand in his trial
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu promised defiantly on Tuesday to knock down corruption allegations against him as he began testifying in his long-running trial, becoming the first sitting Israeli leader to take the stand as a criminal defendant.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syrians in Germany worried by some politicians' eagerness for them to go home after Assad's fall
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Nearly a decade after he arrived in Germany from Syria and took a selfie with then-Chancellor Angela Merkel, Anas Modamani has finished his university studies and has a German passport.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER and GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump names former CIA official Ron Johnson as ambassador to Mexico
item.description: President-elect Trump on Tuesday announced his one-time ambassador to El Salvador, Ron Johnson, would serve as ambassador to Mexico in his upcoming administration.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Trump names Kimberly Guilfoyle ambassador to Greece
item.description: President-elect Trump named Kimberly Guilfoyle as ambassador to Greece in his upcoming administration. Guilfoyle is a former Fox News personality and has been a staple of the Trump family for years since she previously was in a relationship with Donald Trump Jr. The two reportedly have broken up.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Biden is rushing aid to Ukraine. Both sides are digging in. And everyone is bracing for Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The grinding war between Ukraine and its Russian invaders has escalated ahead of Donald Trump's inauguration, with President Joe Biden rushing out billions of dollars more in military aid before U.S. support for Kyiv’s defenses is thrown into question under the new administration.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, ILLIA NOVIKOV and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 1,500-year-old Ten Commandments tablet set for auction
item.description: The tablet has twenty lines of text but only nine of the commandments, with "Thou shalt not take the name of the Lord in vain" omitted.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Citizens against smog: Protesters in North Macedonia demand action against pollution
item.description: SKOPJE, North Macedonia (AP) — Thousands of protesters across North Macedonia took part in demonstrations Tuesday to demand better air quality in cities that are among the most polluted in Europe.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Collapse of Syria’s Assad regime renews US push to find Austin Tice
item.description: The fall of the Bashar al-Assad and the mass liberation of numerous notorious prisons in Syria has brought renewed hope for the location and return of American Austin Tice, held captive for 12 years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea's ex-defense minister is formally arrested over brief imposition of martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea's previous defense minister was formally arrested Wednesday over his alleged collusion with President Yoon Suk Yeol and others in imposing martial law last week, as authorities investigate whether their acts amount to rebellion.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico chews on Kari Lake ambassador rumors
item.description: Reports of President-elect Trump’s potential nomination of Kari Lake as ambassador to Mexico are receiving mixed reviews in the country, where rumors of the appointment have swirled for weeks. Lake, a hard-line MAGA Republican who lost the open Senate race in Arizona to Sen.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: France flies out 2 Mirage fighter jets from Chad to signal beginning of military withdrawal
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France flew out the two Mirage fighter jets it had stationed in Chad on Tuesday, signaling the beginning of its withdrawal of military forces from the former French colony in central Africa that last month broke off its defense cooperation agreement with Paris.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Belarus has dozens of Russian nuclear weapons and is ready for its newest missile, its leader says
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Belarus' authoritarian President Alexander Lukashenko said Tuesday his country is hosting dozens of Russian nuclear weapons and will prepare facilities for the planned deployment of Moscow's newest hypersonic ballistic missile.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazilian President Lula in ICU after surgery to drain head hemorrhage, hospital says
item.description: Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva is recovering in an ICU after undergoing surgery to drain an intracranial hemorrhage following a fall at home in October.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 8 killed in building collapse in Cairo
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — An apartment building collapsed on Tuesday in Egypt’s capital, killing at least eight people, authorities said. The Health Ministry said in a statement that the collapse of the six-story building in Cairo’s western neighborhood of Waili also injured three people, who were hospitalized.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Syria's unaccounted for chemical weapons 'extremely worrying,' UN official says
item.description: Syrian dictator Bashar al-Assad was finally toppled after 13 years of civil war, having ruled Syria since 2000. The world's attention turns to Assad's unaccounted stockpile of chemical weapons.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chinese military makes massive deployment around Taiwan to send 'very simple' message
item.description: Taiwan is raising concerns about a massive deployment of Chinese naval ships and military planes in the past 24 hours, saying the build-up could eventually lead to war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden seems to take credit for Assad's downfall amid fears of Islamic State revival
item.description: President Biden angered some critics when he seemed to claim his administration's Mideast policies led to the downfall of Syria's Bashar Assad on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
